sourceTitle: The 2021 WHO Classification of Tumors of the Central Nervous System
preview: Reclassifies adult-type diffuse gliomas; glioblastoma now defined strictly as IDH-wildtype, WHO grade 4.

sourceTitle: Radiotherapy plus concomitant and adjuvant temozolomide for glioblastoma (Stupp et al., NEJM 2005)
preview: Median OS 12.1 → 14.6 months; 2-year OS 10.4% → 26.5%. The Stupp protocol.

sourceTitle: MGMT gene silencing and benefit from temozolomide in glioblastoma (Hegi et al., NEJM 2005)
preview: Methylated MGMT promoter → 2-year OS 46% with RT + TMZ; primary predictive biomarker for TMZ benefit.

sourceTitle: TTFields + Maintenance TMZ vs TMZ Alone in Glioblastoma (Stupp et al., JAMA 2017, EF-14)
preview: Median OS 20.9 vs 16.0 months; 5-year OS 13% vs 5%. First non-surgical, non-radiation intervention to extend OS since 2005.

sourceTitle: Nivolumab vs Bevacizumab in Recurrent Glioblastoma (CheckMate 143, JAMA Oncol 2020)
preview: Negative trial — checkpoint blockade alone does not extend survival in recurrent GBM.
